title: Use TypeScript strict mode
description: Always enable strict mode in TypeScript
rationale: Catches more errors at compile time
author: test-user

title: EPAM login is the canonical broker
description: Provider bridge flows are temporary until EPAM login provisions provider credentials
rationale: Separate long-term EPAM brokered auth from temporary provider-native auth workarounds
pattern_to_avoid: Manual provider-only login as the permanent model
approved_alternative: Use epam login to provision and store provider credentials locally
author: bjerome

title: EPAM CLI Provider Auth Direction for v1
description: Define the v1 provider auth model as a temporary bridge model, relying on user-provided API keys or provider-native mechanics where applicable, while deferring the long-term EPAM brokered local credential provisioning to future versions.
rationale: Allows immediate usage of the CLI across multiple providers by leveraging existing API key patterns or provider-native browser logins, rather than blocking on the complex implementation of central EPAM credential brokering. Non-goals for v1: central credential provisioning via 'epam login', auto-generated brokered keys.
pattern_to_avoid: Blocking v1 on complex EPAM brokered auto-generated keys
approved_alternative: Use manually provided API keys or provider-native bridge logins for v1 as a short-term workaround
author: product-architect

title: Provider Authentication v1 Bridge Model — Manual Keys + PKCE
description: EPAM CLI v1 will support provider authentication via manual API key entry (BYOK) and browser PKCE flows where available (Codemie), deferring auto-provisioned EPAM-brokered credentials to v2+. This enables immediate multi-provider usage without blocking on complex central credential provisioning infrastructure.
rationale: Competitive analysis shows four distinct auth models: (1) Anthropic Claude Code uses Claude.ai subscription login OR Console API keys, (2) OpenCode uses pure BYOK manual entry, (3) Cursor uses browser login + custom keys with team management, (4) OpenAI Codex uses ChatGPT SSO with auto-generated CLI-scoped keys. Of these, ONLY manual API key entry and generic OAuth PKCE are directly implementable by EPAM CLI without provider-specific backend support. Claude.ai subscription login and ChatGPT SSO auto-keys require provider cooperation not available to third-party CLIs. Team-managed workspace keys require EPAM platform backend enhancements (future work). Therefore, v1 adopts the OpenCode BYOK model as the primary bridge, supplemented by Codemie PKCE (already working), while preserving DEC-003 long-term direction toward EPAM-brokered credential provisioning.
pattern_to_avoid: Blocking v1 on Claude.ai subscription login, ChatGPT SSO auto-keys, or team-managed workspace keys — these require provider-native APIs or EPAM platform backend work not ready for v1
approved_alternative: Use manual API key entry via OS credential manager (KeychainKeyStore) for Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini/Qwen, browser PKCE for Codemie, and env var fallback for CI/CD. v2+ will layer EPAM-brokered central provisioning on top of this foundation.
author: product-architect
